```javascript
const { exec } = require('child_process');
const { promisify } = require('util');

const execAsync = promisify(exec);

/**
 * Escapes special shell characters to prevent command injection
 * @param {string} input - User-provided input to escape
 * @returns {string} - Escaped input safe for shell commands
 */
function escapeShellArg(input) {
    if (typeof input !== 'string') {
        throw new TypeError('Input must be a string');
    }
    
    // Escape single quotes by wrapping in single quotes and escaping existing single quotes
    return "'" + input.replace(/'/g, "'\\''") + "'";
}

/**
 * Validates that the input contains only safe characters for file paths and prefixes
 * @param {string} input - Input to validate
 * @param {string} fieldName - Name of the field for error messages
 */
function validateInput(input, fieldName) {
    if (!input || typeof input !== 'string' || input.length === 0) {
        throw new Error(`${fieldName} must be a non-empty string`);
    }
    
    // Allow alphanumeric characters, hyphens, underscores, and dots
    // Explicitly exclude wildcard characters (*, [, ?) and path separators
    const safePattern = /^[a-zA-Z0-9_.\-]+$/;
    
    if (!safePattern.test(input)) {
        throw new Error(`${fieldName} contains invalid characters. Only alphanumeric characters, hyphens, underscores, and dots are allowed.`);
    }
    
    // Prevent directory traversal attempts
    if (input.includes('..') || input.includes('/')) {
        throw new Error(`${fieldName} cannot contain directory traversal sequences or path separators`);
    }
}

/**
 * Searches for shell script files with a specific prefix in a directory and counts
 * lines containing the 'sudo' command
 * 
 * @param {string} searchDir - The directory to search in
 * @param {string} filePrefix - The prefix for shell script filenames
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - The count of lines containing 'sudo'
 */
async function analyzeSudoUsageInScripts(searchDir, filePrefix) {
    // Validate inputs
    validateInput(searchDir, 'searchDir');
    validateInput(filePrefix, 'filePrefix');
    
    try {
        // Escape the inputs for safe shell usage
        const escapedSearchDir = escapeShellArg(searchDir);
        
        // Build the command with proper escaping
        // Using -F for fixed strings (not regex) to avoid regex injection
        const command = `find ${escapedSearchDir} -name "${filePrefix}*.sh" -type f -exec grep -c -F "sudo" {} + 2>/dev/null`;
        
        // Execute the command
        const { stdout, stderr } = await execAsync(command);
        
        // Parse the result (grep -c returns the count for each file)
        const lines = stdout.trim().split('\n').filter(line => line.length > 0);
        
        // Sum up all the counts from grep -c
        const totalCount = lines.reduce((sum, line) => {
            const count = parseInt(line, 10);
            return sum + (isNaN(count) ? 0 : count);
        }, 0);
        
        return totalCount;
        
    } catch (error) {
        // Handle specific error cases
        if (error.code === 1) {
            // grep returns exit code 1 when no matches found
            return 0;
        }
        
        // Log error for debugging but don't expose sensitive details
        console.error('Error analyzing sudo usage in scripts:', error.message);
        
        // Re-throw with a user-friendly message
        throw new Error('Failed to analyze script files. Please check the directory path and try again.');
    }
}

module.exports = { analyzeSudoUsageInScripts };
```

```javascript
/**
 * Analyzes sudo usage in shell script files by counting lines containing 'sudo'
 * @param {string} searchDir - Directory to search for scripts
 * @param {string} filePrefix - Prefix for script filenames
 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Count of lines containing 'sudo'
 */
function analyzeSudoUsageInScripts(searchDir, filePrefix) {
    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
        // Validate input parameters
        if (!searchDir || typeof searchDir !== 'string') {
            return reject(new Error('searchDir must be a non-empty string'));
        }
        if (!filePrefix || typeof filePrefix !== 'string') {
            return reject(new Error('filePrefix must be a non-empty string'));
```

```xml
const { promisify } = require('util');

const execAsync = promisify(exec);

/**
 * Escapes a string for safe use as a shell argument.
 * Wraps the string in single quotes and escapes any existing single quotes.
 * E.g., "foo; bar" becomes "'foo; bar'".
 *
 * @param {string} arg - The argument to escape

 * @returns {Promise<number>} - Count of lines containing the 'sudo' command

async function analyzeSudoUsageInScripts(searchDir, filePrefix) {
    if (!searchDir || typeof searchDir !== 'string') {
```

```javascript
const { exec } = require('child_process');

function escapeShell(str) {
    return str.replace(/([\\$`!"'])/g, '\\$1');

function analyzeSudoUsageInScripts(searchDir, filePrefix) {
    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {

        if (!searchDir || typeof searchDir !== 'string' || !searchDir.trim()) {
            reject(new Error('Invalid search directory'));
```
